what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card with a two-column brand logo display: the black ConceptV wordmark on a light canvas pane and the yellow V mark on a dark pane.
observations: Includes a @dsCard metadata comment defining group, name, subtitle, and viewport.
Loads shared card styling from _card.css and adds file-local CSS for the preview layout.
Uses CSS variables for canvas, dark background, borders, muted text, and font family.
Displays two labeled panes: "On canvas · primary" and "On dark · use V mark".
References two logo image assets with relative paths: conceptv-wordmark-black.png and conceptv-v-yellow.png.
The dark-pane logo sizing is set with an inline style on the img element.
standouts: The preview encodes a background-dependent brand asset rule directly in the rendered example.
The card is split into equal light and dark panes for side-by-side comparison.
The @dsCard comment makes the file discoverable as a Brand preview artifact.
conventions: Uses a standalone HTML preview file with embedded CSS and shared _card.css dependency.
Follows design-token-style CSS variables for colors and typography.
Uses semantic alt text for the logo images.
Uses class-based pane variants for light and dark states, with one inline override for the dark logo image.
concerns: The CSS comment says the dark pane replaces the wordmark via CSS, but the markup directly uses a different image asset.
The inline style on the dark-pane image duplicates sizing concerns outside the stylesheet.
notes: This file is a compact brand preview showing which ConceptV logo asset appears on light versus dark backgrounds.
questions: Should the dark-pane asset swap be considered a markup choice or a CSS-driven replacement as the comment suggests?
Are the 700x260 viewport and 96px V-mark height fixed requirements for this preview?
Should the visible labels be part of the preview contract or only local annotation?
Is the yellow V mark the intended dark-background brand asset in all dark contexts or only in this card?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated @dsCard purpose matches the rendered content: it shows the real wordmark on canvas and switches to the V mark on dark.
intention_signals: It appears intended to document and demonstrate ConceptV brand mark usage across light and dark surfaces in a reusable preview-card format.
novelty: The file demonstrates a rule that the dark-background treatment uses the standalone V mark rather than simply recoloring the full wordmark.
contribution: Contributes a file-local example of background-specific ConceptV logo usage, including preview metadata, side-by-side light/dark presentation, label styling, and concrete asset references for wordmark and V-mark contexts.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML design-system card preview rendering the ConceptV black wordmark on a light canvas beside the yellow V mark on a dark pane with token-based styling.
intention_for_embedding: Created to document and visually demonstrate the appropriate ConceptV brand asset for light canvas and dark background contexts.
